reading: Constructor-shared degradation is unbiased on average (mean slope +0.0030 s/lap-age, ~0); but per-pair dispersion (noise 0.0410) exceeds the reordering signal (0.0156), SNR=0.38. Per-stint deg reorderings are within noise — trust full-race aggregation, not single-lap deltas.

Switchers MAE 1.457 vs stayers 1.850; gap -0.394 (95% CI [-1.063, +0.320]). CI spans 0 → no evidence the team change adds transfer error (supports car-independence), but UNDERPOWERED on this checkout's coverage.

Model Spearman 0.600 (top-3 0.47) vs host-invariant baseline 0.001 (top-3 0.12); gap +0.600 (95% CI [+0.534, +0.663]). Car/track structure beats skill-only — criterion 2 PASS. Skill-only ~ random confirms the constructor pace the host-swap manipulates carries the finish-order signal.

Full-pairwise: resolution present (Brier 0.234 vs 0.5-baseline 0.250) but OVERCONFIDENT — slope 0.70 (target ~1), predicts 0.80 delivers 0.66. Inflating the predicted SE by k=2.77 restores slope 0.91. Mart adjacent p_beats_next (app-facing, hardest pairs) is uninformative: slope 0.57, Brier 0.270. Criterion 3 (slope ~= 1) NOT met as-shipped — needs SE inflation before live.

Core CORRECT: MC reproduces analytic p_beats_next (max dev 0.025 ~ MC error 0.014), p_position is a valid distribution (row/col sums to 1 within 4e-16), expected position monotone in pace (min Spearman 0.98). BUT finish_pos_se diverges from the mart's Poisson-binomial (median 2.35, p95 3.33, max 4.40 positions): pairwise-independence understates true position uncertainty under high pace overlap. The MC (permutation-respecting) is the reference — corroborates 4.4's overconfidence; the live engine should take finish-position SE from the MC, not the analytic approximation.
